entry.title: Enable Now experience for content authors after migration to Cloud
entry.content:
Looking for information on the user experience for content creators after on-prem version of Enable Now is migrated to the cloud. 

Is Producer still used in the same manner, in conjunction with Enable Now Manager?

What is the log-in experience to create content? 

Are work areas mapped differently?

Is there a difference to how content is created?

I can find a lot of info on back end processes to connect the tool, but I am looking for details on the experience specifically for content creators. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Community Member Spotlight: Roberta Fernandes - February 2025 Edition
entry.content: Que jornada incrível, estou começando no SAP e foi maravilhoso ler cada linha desta entrevista.

entry.title: dblog and DFS share
entry.content:
SQLA 17.0.11.7821 for Windows:

dblog always reports "file... not found" when trying to get information about a DB file using dblog.exe and the file path pointing to a DFS share. The file exists and is accessible by other programs.

Trying the same with a file on an UNC share works, but a DFS share fails always. 

Any advice? Is dblog not compatible with DFS shares?

entry.title: SAP Business Application Studio?
entry.content: Whether ABAP applications can be developed with SAP Business Application Studio...?

entry.title: Re: #ABAP_Trial : SAP ABAP Platform 1909 - Start FIORI Launchpad
entry.content: Hi, Docker Container [ https://hub.docker.com/_/sap-abap-trial/plans/ac8a4f9b-ae29-4afa-9b39-25aeea24b821?tab=instructions ] NOT working at all. Thanks Kushal

entry.title: Is it possible to read the data from analytical view and write it back to SAC planning model
entry.content:
Hi,

Is it possible to read the data from analytical view from SAP datasphere and write it back to SAC planning model ?

I know we can do this by importing the data from SAP Datasphere to SAC via Odata connection but is it possible via scripting?

Anyone who has faced this issue or knows the workaround in this case. Their comments would be deeply appreciated.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP MDK - Android - Inquiry About Font Size Handl...
entry.content: I see you opened a support ticket for this same information.  We will respond on the ticket.

entry.title: Re: Crystal Report Viewer inside UpdatePanel displays onl...
entry.content: Thank you for your response. We are already storing the report in session and reassign it on postback. There is no issue on loading the report source to report viewer, because we are able to print/export the report from viewer even if it's not displayed properly.

entry.title: Re: SAC Advance Formula Error while copying data from one...
entry.content:
@VikasB 

If [d/BASECODE] is common between 2 models why are you qualifying it with model name. May be try as below.

 

DATA(
    [d/Measures] = "MDP", 
[d/ESTIMATE_CODE] = [d/BASECODE].[p/EST_CODE],
[d/FACTORY] = [d/BASECODE].[p/FACTORY],
    [d/SaFARI_PRODUCT] = [[d/BASECODE].[p/PRODUCT]
 
) = LINK(
    [SaFARI - PTA Actuals],
    [d/Version] = "public.Actual",
    [d/Measures] = "MDP"
)

entry.title: Re: Can we control Custom Logic code using Sy-Tcode or Ap...
entry.content:
Ah ok

I think the standard is to use the sy-tcode field

 

DATA: lv_tcode TYPE sy-tcode.

" Get the calling transaction code
lv_tcode = sy-tcode.

 

 

However, S/4HANA Cloud uses a restricted abap called "Abap for key users", according to the help in S/4HANA Cloud public edition, that field is not listed.

But please try it and let us know if this works for you.

Versions:

https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_latest_index_htm/latest/en-US/abenabap_versions.htm

 

Abap for key users

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/dd39f11e977b41b49a5421399f4850f5.html?locale=en-US

 

I hope this helps

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: RFC Channel - Ping test fails - Cache updated successfully
entry.content:
Hi Friends, 

Good day. 

In the existing RFC receiver channel (PIPO 7.5), I changed user id and password to connect to ECC and debug ABAP code there when data is sent from PI. 

The channel was activated successfully, and my new user id is shown in Cache Monitor (PIMON). i.e CPA Cache was updated successfully. 

However, when I ping the RFC channel in Channel Monitoring, it gives the following error message.

Error during channel ping caused by: com.sap.conn.jco.JCoException: (103) JCO_ERROR_LOGON_FAILURE: Initialization of destination RfcClient_aadfd0919acc3684aaf5bd196887yca6 failed: Name or password is incorrect (repeat logon).

i.e the Ping channel considered the old username and password. It did not consider the new username and password. This is surprising because in Cache Monitoring this channel shows new user id. 

I stopped and started RFC channel, did delta cache refresh, restarted AIF core service & CPA Cache service in NWA. Nothing is helped to find out the issue. 

When I created a new RFC channel with the new user id and credentials, it works fine. 

Could you please clarify?

Thank you. 

Regards, 

Jegatheeswaran P

entry.title: Extension field extension scenario in S4 Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Experts ,

Can anyone help me with the Extension field extension scenario in S4 Public Cloud .

which means I want to create extension fields on Material Master and extend it to purchase and sales orders.

I know about "Custom Field" app and tried the possibility there. In that I didn't find the business context to support my requirement.

Do someone have the similar requirement or solution from past ?

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP MDK - Android - Inquiry About Font Size Handl...

entry.title: Cannot use custom CDS view on datasphere replication flow
entry.content:
Hello.

I've created a custom CDS view (a copy paste from the standard CDS C_SALESCHANGEDOCITEMDEX):

However, when trying to use this CDS on a replication flow, is failling (with no details on the error):

When the replication flow uses the standard CDS view (C_SALESCHANGEDOCITEMDEX), there is no errors:

No errors on ST22 or autorization problems on SU53.

Thanks in advance for any help.

entry.title: Re: Close Group Tree button is hidden in Crystal report v...
entry.content: Thank you for your response. I had a look on this link and we are already using session and postback.

entry.title: Re: Let's get to know each other!
entry.content:
Hi Carl!

Welcome to the group, and thanks for introducing yourself! It’s great to hear about your transformation journey with SAP Signavio—big changes like these can feel overwhelming at first, but you’re in the right place to connect with peers who’ve been through it.

I also wanted to invite you to an SAP Signavio Community Expert Session on March 19, 2025, that I think you’ll find valuable:

The Role of Business Process Management During Your SAP S/4HANA Transformation Journey

This session will dive into how business process management can guide your transformation with practical insights on SAP Activate and best practices. It’s a great opportunity to ask questions, learn from experts, and connect with others tackling similar challenges.

Also, since you’re just getting started, you might find the SAP Signavio Onboarding Resource Center helpful—it’s a great place to explore key resources and best practices to get up to speed faster.

Would love to see you at the session! Let me know if you have any questions.

Best,
Samuel

entry.title: Re: How to edit Product group, Product group type after SAP S/4 HANA 2023 release
entry.content: Great, thanks for the knowledge.

entry.title: Re: 🇦🇹 Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model and SAP generative AI H
entry.content:
Dear SAP community, dear developers,
We are still looking for a suitable location for the CodeJam, preferably at a favourable price. If any of you have access to a venue with tables, chairs, sockets, wifi and a projector, please let us know. 
From the community, for the community. 💪
Thx!!!

entry.title: Do you want to acquire foundational knowledge of SAP Business Data Cloud?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Introducing SAP Business Data Cloud learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey provides you with an introduction to SAP Business Data Cloud.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, you will have acquired foundational knowledge of SAP Business Data Cloud. You will discover key components of SAP Business Data Cloud and their main tasks. You will also gain an insight into SAP Business Data Cloud use cases and explore innovative SAP Business Data Cloud features. 

Prerequisites

  • Basic knowledge of data & analytics 

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Managing Characteristic Changes for a Single SFC in SAP DM
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to execute a process using SAP DM, and the scenario is as follows:

I have an SFC, which inherits characteristics from its main order. One of these characteristics may change based on an analysis conducted on the shop floor. Even if an order generates 100 SFCs, I want to modify the characteristic for only one specific SFC and confirm the process accordingly.

Potential Solution:

As far as I know, batch characteristics can be modified at the SFC level. The batch characteristics assigned to the order are copied to each SFC upon creation. If necessary, I can update the characteristic for a single SFC in this manner, allowing ERP to recognize the change through standard processes.

Ultimately, while I have a potential solution, I would like to know if there is a more efficient way to manage this process using SAP DM standards.

Thank you in advance for your help and insights.

Best regards,
MSY

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to read the data from analytical view...
entry.content:
@azm_azm 

There is no scripting API to support Writeback to planning model. The Analytic Application API are more for layout and UX and not data writeback. Yes Importing data is an option as you have mentioned.

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Implementing FIFO Tracking in SAP DM
entry.content:
Hello,

I am working on a process in SAP DM, and the scenario is as follows:

A steel billet with a unique ID enters the furnace, and at the output, five different products are created from a single billet. To track which product originates from which billet, I need to establish a FIFO structure. However, I have not been able to find a solution for implementing this FIFO structure within SAP DM.

I would greatly appreciate any insights or possible solutions you can provide on this matter.

Thank you in advance for your help and feedback.

Best regards,
MSY

entry.title: Account to roll forward to another one?
entry.content:
Trying to make it as simple as can, there are GL Account A, B, C and D

The request is to the balance of B, C and D GL accounts roll forward to account A when we perform the yearly balance carry forward.

Is that possible? And how?

entry.title: F1519 "Schedule Billing Creation" app: can a custom field (e.g. invoice value) be added?
entry.content:
I would like to add a custom field to the invoice data selection criteria of the app F1519 "Schedule Billing Creation". The goal is to only pick documents with value zero and create billing documents automatically.

The selection criteria looks like a "Fiorized" VF06 which from my GUI-days experience could only be extended via enhancement points. Also SAP Guided Answers say that the app has the type "SAP Fiori: Generic Job Scheduling Framework" which cannot be extended. However, in the Extensibility section of the Fiori Reference library the option "Extensibility via SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects" is mentioned. 

Does anybody have an experience extending such Fiori apps?

entry.title: The fastest way to a deployable CAP app on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
So, you have developed your CAP app locally and are wondering: What's the fastest way to get this on the BTP?

Or, if you are like me, you sometimes want to try something out in a deployed version quickly.

Even if things are running smoothly in your local environment, correctly binding and bundling everything up into a working cloud app, that is connected to the database, handles authorization etc. can be cumbersome.

However, CAP has a few tools, that simplify this a lot.

For me the shortest way to get an application deployed is the following:

After logging on to cf using the cli with 

 

cf l --sso

 

I execute the following in the shell:

 

cds init fast_deploy
cd fast_deploy
cds add sample
cds add mta,hana,xsuaa --for production
cds add workzone
npm i
npm i --prefix app/admin-books
npm i --prefix app/browse
mbt build -t gen --mtar mta.tar
cf deploy gen/mta.tar

 

 Given that there is a working BTP subaccount configured with the correct entitlements as well as HANA Cloud and Workzone ready, this will:

Line1: Initialise a new empty CAP project

Line3: Add a sample application with domain model, service definition and two sample Fiori apps (if you are doing this in your own project you can as well just skip those initial three lines.)

Line 4+5: Let the cds dk add all the relevant configurations and specify

  • SAP HANA as the target database
  • xsuaa as the identity service
  • the `mta.yaml` file which describes how all of this should be deployed
  • SAP BTP Workzone as the entry page holding the tiles for your Fiori apps

Line 6-8: Installing the dependencies for the CAP app and the two Fiori apps.

Line 9: Building the MTA 

Line 10: Deploying it to Cloud Foundry

So, with 10 lines in the shell, we have created an app, added all dependencies, bundled it up and deployed it to the BTP.

All that is left is to configure the app to show up on the Workzone (updating the content channel, add the apps in the content manager and assign them to a role and a group) after which you can also access the Fiori apps.

And for getting rid of the application without manually removing service keys and instances, just use the following:

 

cf undeploy fast_deploy --delete-services --delete-service-keys

 

That's it. For any developer who ever spent a few hours trying to manually adjust configurations in yaml or json files while getting a new error message after each redeployment... this should help make life easier 😀

All the best

Daniel

entry.title: Re: Crystal Report Viewer inside UpdatePanel displays onl...
entry.content: What SP are you using? You can get the latest SP37 from here:https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html

entry.title: S_PROGNAM Authorization check always successful
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I activated the scenario BC_GENERIC_REPORT_START using transaction SACF, which enabled the authorization check for S_PROGNAM. When users attempt to run a critical program without the necessary authorization in S_PROGNAM, they receive an authorization warning (not an error!). Despite the warning message, after confirming it, they are still able to access the initial screen of the report.

The failed authorization checks related to these warnings are visible in SU53 or STAUTHTRACE. However, my goal is to prevent users from accessing the report altogether once they receive and confirming the warning message. Instead, they should be immediately redirected out of SA38. How can I implement that? Below are several screenshots for the issue I'm talking about.

When an end user tries to run a report for which they do not have the necessary authorization,

 they receive the following warning:

However, if they click on "Continue," they are still able to access the initial screen of the report:

I want to prevent this from happening. Instead, they should be immediately redirected out of SA38. Do you know how I can implement this?

Best Regards,

Alp

entry.title: Length issue for company name in CBC
entry.content:
We recently need to change the legal name of the companies. We detected the company name length is only 30 characters, Is there a way to extend this ? 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Length issue for company name in CBC
entry.content:
While creating the Organizational Structure in CBC The Company name length is up to 30 characters only.

To extend the Company name please check the below KBA:

3451268 - How to enter company name more than 25 characters - SAP S/4HANA Cloud? - SAP for Me

entry.title: Acquiring Core ABAP Skills - learning examples (package - /LRN/S4D400_EXERCISE)
entry.content:
@Margit_Wagner 
Hello Margit,
please could you guide me a bit ? I am following learning path Acquiring Core ABAP Skills. I settled BTP learning server, connect it with Eclipse and so on. Everything is working. But, when I want check the learning examples from the package /LRN/S4D400_EXERCISE I can not. This package is not available in this server. Do I have to set something ? Because I have got subscription for 6 attempts as you can see on the screenshot below. /DMO/Flight is there but the first one mentioned not. I am trying to follow up the learning path without that, but it is really annoying that i can not see the examples, which are mentioned in the learning guidelines.

Thank you in advance 
KR 
Tim

entry.title: Re: 'Free' text from classroom trainings registration
entry.content: @JyotiA, the "showPricingInternalUsers"/"showPricingExternalUsers" settings can be reviewed (KBA 2228338). Please note the following details available right above these settings in the "FINANCIAL" area: "When set to false, catalog search will not include currency filter or price sorting and prices will not be displayed in user catalog, user registration and available offerings. This configuration does not turn off commerce functionality and should not be set to false if any learning has a Price associated for the respective user group.". Alternatively, the "Free" text label value could be reviewed (KBA 2120336).

entry.title: Re: Crystal Report Viewer inside UpdatePanel displays onl...
entry.content: We are using the latest one - SP37

entry.title: Re: Community Member Spotlight: Roberta Fernandes - February 2025 Edition
entry.content: Well done @RobertaFernande and keep up the fantastic work!

entry.title: SAP CAP Node.js - Insert vector embedding data into hana cloud is not working
entry.content:
Hello, everyone,

I referred these two links cap-ai-vector-engine-sample ,cap-llm-plugin-samples  to develop a CAP RAG application.

When I insert the embedding data into the db, I got an error message "argument type mismatch: Result is not a single value".

After my research, I found that if the dependency @SAP/cds-hana is added, data can be inserted into the database successfully, However, my App use Fiori elements and enbaled draft function, if the @SAP/cds-hana dependency is used, my Fiori app will encounter an error: SqlError: invalid column name: DRAFTADMINISTRATIVEDATA.INPROCESSBYUSER. When this dependency is removed, the Fiori app will not report an error, but I won't be able to insert data into the db. It seems that this is a dilemma, So, is there a conflict between @SAP/cds-hana and the draft-enabled function?  And is there any way to insert data into the db normally without using this dependency?

Any advice or suggestions would be appreciated.

schema.cds

entity DocumentChunk : cuid, managed {
    file            : Association to Files;
    text_chunk      : LargeString;
    metadata_column : LargeString;
    embedding       : Vector(1536);
}

service.js ( insert into db )

const embeddingResult = await capllmplugin.getEmbeddingWithConfig(
          embeddingModelConfig,
          chunk.pageContent
        );

embedding = embeddingResult?.data[0]?.embedding;

const entry = {
          file_ID: uuid,
          text_chunk: chunk.pageContent,
          metadata_column: fileName,
          embedding: array2VectorBuffer(embedding),
        };
textChunkEntries.push(entry);

// Insert the text chunk with embeddings into db
      const insertStatus = await INSERT.into(DocumentChunk).entries(
        textChunkEntries
      );

package.json

 "dependencies": {
    "@sap-cloud-sdk/http-client": "3.25.0",
    "@sap-cloud-sdk/resilience": "3.25.0",
    "@sap/cds": "^8",
    "@cap-js/hana": "^1",
    "@sap/xssec": "^4",
    "cap-llm-plugin": "^1.4.4",
    "express": "^4",
    "langchain": "^0.1.19",
  }

done, hopefully I did it properly. I have never done it before.
Thank you 
KR
Tim

entry.title: Re: SAC Advance Formula Error while copying data from one...

entry.title: JBOSS 8 Compability
entry.content:
We are currently using JBoss EAP 7 JAVA 8 to host an application that uses the SAP Crystal Reports, developer version for Eclipse Runtime libraries. 

We need to upgrade to JBoss EAP 8 JAVA 17 however the latest SP 31 Runtime libraries do not seem to be compatible with it. We are getting JAVA namespace issues. 

Is there an alternative Runtime libraries that uses the modern JAVA namespaces?

The current namespaces are Jakarta EE 8 and JBOSS EAP 8 uses Jakarta EE 10 API namespace

entry.title: RCM Job Application picklist value
entry.content: RCM Job Application picklist value  modified but still showing old values  in application drop down for candidate. And newly added values also not showing. How we can update it.Tried available all method but not working.

entry.title: SAP Armenia Academic Community Roundtable at ASUE
entry.content:
The SAP University Alliances cordially invite you to participate in SAP Armenia Academic Community Roundtable held on 25th February, 2025, at the Armenian State University of Economics (ASUE). The agenda includes welcome remarks by ASUE Vice Rector Davit Hakhverdyan, SAP University Alliance Partners Success Director Michael Nuernberg, presentations by academic community and business partners, and Roundtable discussion.

Location: ASUE Main Building, Erasmus Hall, Nalbandyan Str., 128, Yerevan

NOTE: To confirm your participation please click "Going" 

Time

Name, surname

Organization

Presentation

11:00-11:15

Davit Hakhverdyan  

Michael Nuernberg

ASUE Vice Rector

VP, SAP University Alliances

Welcome remarks

11:15-11:30

Valeri Sargsyan

SAP Armenia/Georgia Managing Director

SAP Business Overview

11:30-11:45

Igor Belousov

VP, SAP University Alliances

SAP University Alliances new education offerings

11:45-12:00

 

Revytech

SAP projects and skillsets needed

12:00-12:15

Bella Vasilyan

 

Ruben Mirzoyan

Director TUMO Labs

 

Senior Project Manager TUMO

TUMO Labs: overview, collaboration with SAP, current projects

12:15-12:30

Ararat Zaqaryan

OneVision

SAP projects in Armenia

12:30-12:45

Sos Khachikyan

ASUE, Director of Interdisciplinary Center for Digitalization and Sustainable Development

 

 

Sos Khachikyan

 

ASUE

 

12:45-13:15

Igor Belousov

SAP University Alliances

Round Table Discussion – all participants

entry.title: Re: Prevent reprinting the Payment Advice in F110 on S4HC?
entry.content: Thank you for your answer, but need is. Is there any possibility to display the PDF File in only one time . Thanks

entry.title: Re: Settlement Document: Tax Code and Tax determination i...
entry.content:
Hi @Pankaj_M, 


To enable automatic tax code determination from pricing in manual settlement documents (0S32), you need to:

  1. Copy the standard Settlement Process Type and modify the configuration to change the relevant entry from "Manual Entry" to "3 - From Price Determination". This ensures that the system automatically picks up the tax code and amount from pricing.

  2. Alternative Approach – Transfer Manager

    • You can use Transfer Manager to default the tax code during settlement document creation.
    • If additional logic is required, you may need to implement custom logic to determine the tax code dynamically.

Since you are using Vertex for tax determination, ensure that it is properly integrated with SAP Settlement Management and can retrieve all relevant data for tax determination. Typically, Vertex uses fields like:

  • Ship-to Party
  • Sold-to Party

However, these fields do not exist in a Settlement Document by default. You may need to explore custom solutions to ensure that the required tax-relevant data is available to Vertex at the time of settlement.

Let me know if you need further clarification!

Best regards,
Gauthier

entry.title: How to control the rate of production for a multi resource in PP/DS
entry.content:
Hello
We have multi resources setup in ePP/DS, now users want to control the rate of production by adjusting the number of capacities. By going through standard documentation, I understand that only utilization rate can affect the resource rate. Can someone please confirm or provide some alternatives for this requirement.

entry.title: Cost Center in TRM Financial Transactions
entry.content:
This article describes how to use cost center in financial transactions in Treasury and Risk Management (TRM) of in S/4HANA Public Cloud, mainly focus on how to derive the cost center in posting journals arising from TRM transactions.

1. When creating transactions for some product categories (600, 760, 620), a cost center can be entered in sub-area Additional Fields of Administr. tab.

For example, below is a FX forward transaction (product category 600):

When doing so in the transactions of product category 600 or 760 (OTC FX option), the major purpose is using cost center as a differentiation criterion in hedge management to match the FX exposure and FX derivative at the level of cost center. For more details regarding differentiation criteria in hedge management, please refer to help document https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/e5ec5859d8e54df98492d80564a734c0/618b3d58eaa89144e10000000a4450e5.html?locale=en-US.

The cost center entered in sub-area Additional Fields of Administr. tab will not be reflected in the position indicator of this transaction, and consequently, the cost center will not be carried over to the posting journals generated from the transaction.

2. Through configuration activity 102639 (Manage Default Account Assignments), you can define the default cost centers for cost elements and company codes specifically, and the TRM posting journal line items for those cost elements will have the default cost centers assigned. Please note that the cost center assigned here is not reflected in any part of the TRM transactions, because this configuration take effects during creation of postings in financial accounting.

This method is useful when you have TRM specific cost elements (such as charge, interest income/expense, valuation gain/loss, etc.), and it is reasonable to assign a single default cost center to the postings related to this cost element from multiple TRM transactions, ensuring consistency and simplifying the accounting process.

3. Through configuration activity 105341 (Allocate Additional Account Assignments to Account Assignment References), you can assign a cost center to a combination of company code and account assignment reference (AAR).

Afterwards, when creating a TRM transaction, the cost center can be derived from company code and AAR, and it is stored in the position indicator. Then in the posting journals arising from this transaction, this cost center will be assigned to the cost element related line items by default.

This method is useful when you have multiple AARs, and different AARs should derive their corresponding cost centers.

The level of detail in the cost centers derived using this method actually depends on how detailed the AARs are defined and the preconditions on which the AARs are based. For more details on the derivation of AARs, please refer to the help document https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/e5ec5859d8e54df98492d80564a734c0/713cc111330d4439a7331df96d070e5b.html?locale=en-US.

4. In app Manage Substitution / Validation Rules - Journal Entries, you can create a substitution rule with the business context Coding Block. The preconditions in the substitution rule should be comprehensive enough in order to derive the correct cost center (otherwise it doesn’t have advantage compared to other means), and the preconditions might rely on other substitutions.

Additionally, implementing the BAdI FIN_CODING_BLOCK_SUBSTITUTION might be beneficial as it offers more flexible development extensibility, allowing for customized coding block substitutions to better meet complex business requirements. For details, please refer to help document https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/e5ec5859d8e54df98492d80564a734c0/6d95f6fa596646f59cfb9dd0dd7b7fd3.html?locale=en-US.

Please note that the cost center derived via substitution rule or BAdI implementation is not reflected in any part of the TRM transactions, because they take effects during creation of postings in financial accounting.

5. Through configuration activity 104115 (Define and Assign Differentiations), you can define a differentiation which includes term Cost Center, and then assign this differentiation to proper valuation area(s) and/or accounting code(s).

Afterwards, when creating a financial transaction within the related accounting code or for related valuation area, in the tab Administr., you will see the field Cost Center within the subarea Position Assignment. And this Cost Center field is mandatory to be entered with a value.

The cost center entered in sub-area Position Assignment of Administr. tab is stored in position indicator of this transaction, and consequently, the cost center will be carried over to the cost element related line items of the posting journals generated from this transaction. In the position indicator, the cost center column is grayed out, because it must be identical with the cost center specified in sub-area Position Assignment of Administr. tab.

This method is useful when transaction (of a product category other than 600, 760, 620 which have cost center field opened in tab Administr. by default) requires separate and manual determination of the cost center and store it in tab Administr. of the transaction, and this cost center should be used to record the profit/loss arising from this transaction from accounting perspective.

However, the decision to use Define and Assign Differentiations should be made cautiously, as it not only makes the Cost Center visible in transactions but also has several significant impacts on transaction processing and accounting, which need to be thoroughly evaluated before configuration:

  • For every transaction within the valuation area and/or accounting code which is assigned with this differentiation, cost center field will be a mandatory field to entered in financial transactions.
  • Position and quantity of financial transactions will be split according to cost centers in addition to predefined differentiations, this effect is especially obvious for financial transactions and class positions of securities (e.g. bond, stock).
  • Because of above point, the profit/loss (for example, realized gain/loss when derecognition) will be measured and accounted in line with each cost center.

6. You may also manually specify a cost center in position indicator (when it is possible). This method can be used in rare circumstances when a different cost center than the one derived via other means is required due to specific exceptions or unique transaction characteristics.

entry.title: Re: CodeJam in Royal Eswatini Sugar Corporation (Eswatini)
entry.content: I'm afraid not.  The CodeJam is designed to be an in person experience and doesn't translate well to virtual.  For virtual learning we have materials available on learning.sap.com, developer tutorials on developers.sap.com, and of course many videos on the SAP Developers YouTube channel.

entry.title: Re: Subcontracting with Chargeable Components - info cate...
entry.content:
Found: need to go to OMESCJ tcode for activate chargeable info record.

It must be done in each client manually.

entry.title: DM Transfer several input parameters to a production process
entry.content:
Hi experts,

we currently have a production process on a button in the operation activity POD. I have mapped the input parameters, also including the operation activity. If we execute the button with several operation activities marked, then only one operation activities is transferred to the production process. I would have expected that the process is done several times with all transferred operation activities or that they would have been transferred in an array as one input parameter.

Does anyone know if it is possible to transfer several operation activities to the production process?

 

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Configuration of Quick Apply in Recruiting. Advantages and Disadvantages
entry.content:
Hi there!

We encountered a similar inquiry from our Talent org, re: candidate pool expansion via shortened application and were informed we could create an entirely new, shortened application template to attach to various areas and generate candidate pools from such. In our situation, we were unable to pursue the option for an amended app template available for different app interfaces (quick apply via various networking sites) because it failed to meet our compliance criteria threshold. We had required questions that in of themselves made the application longer, but couldn't be omitted. 

While the shortened app did assist with faster candidate initial application times, it did require an additional internal business process to handle responses and then engage based off quick apply results. This also required we route through full application for full data receipt, attribute manually to recommended/forwarded apply to requisitions and process from there via our business flow status etc within RCM. We elected not to pursue since the compliance reqs as a security attributed govt/federal contractor prohibited much shortening without a significant uptick in change to our existing candidate profile requirements for basic quals. 

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: Attachments in Project limit
entry.content:
Hi Enda,

Thanks for your reply. As per my understanding limit of 100 MB is per file, but what is the total space available? How many files of 100 Mb can be uploaded?

Thanks and kind regards

Angelina Mastrogiacomo

entry.title: Re: Charts in Report stories are not getting refreshed wi...
entry.content: Hi Olga, The report is built correctly as it provides the right data, only if we give in some sort of date filter in the front end. Else the report even after subsequent refresh does not show the live data. We have 3 reports which are executed to compare teh data, active list, separation and dashboard. When user executes them, the data at the first run will match. Subsequent runs, say mid of the day, we will have more hires and some separations. The active list and separation reports show correct data, while teh dashboard shows the first run data only. It does not get updated or refreshed even when done manually. Hence facing the issue. Should any other setting is required? For time being, i have enabled the setting as per the given KBA https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3503253. However, as per the CXO team, they want the chart to display directly upon single click and data should show live data. Kindly help

entry.title: Re: SAP ILM with SAP IQ Database
entry.content:
Hello Roland Kramer,

Firstly, thank you for the wonderful blog.

We are planning to launch an SAP IQ server for ILM archiving. In our case, we want to archive data from multiple ECC systems and S/4 systems into one SAP IQ. I am wondering how we can segregate the data being archived into one SAP IQ instance from multiple sources. Do we need to create multiple database users in SAP IQ and their respective schemas? Does SAP IQ support the concept of schemas?

Could you please shed some light on this? Any hints or guidance would be much appreciated.

Thanks in advance.

Best regards,
SR

entry.title: Re: Solving RSA1 issue after BW Refresh
entry.content:
"First, we’ll delete all irrelevant entries for TABLE – RSBASIDOC, RSLOGSYSDEST, RSLOGSYSMAP"

Any kind of manual change or modification of RSBASIDOC is not supported. This table should never be touched manually. The only exception is if SAP (support, development) explicitly asks for it. It should never be something, to be done on a regular basis.

If a specific source system is not required anymore, then instead of removing it from RSBASIDOC, just delete the source system itself.

 

There are official documents, that provide detailed descriptions of BW Systemcopy procedures:

The main guide to perform a manual systemcopy with a consistent result is described in the following resources:

  • 886102 - System Landscape Copy for SAP NetWeaver BW
  • BW SystemCopy and SAPI Source System connections
  • 2176213 - Important SAP notes and KBAs for BW System Copy
  • 2572113 - BDLS jobs canceled, "Cannot copy transformation" error occurred
  • 2924111 - [BW Central KBA] BDLS
  • Myself connection
  • How to check a BW <-> SAP source system connection
  • BW SAPI Source Systems - Troubleshooting
  • 2956859 - [BW Central KBA] Troubleshooting SAPI Source Systems Inconsistencies
  • 2005372 - Error messages after system refresh procedure (RSAR, E0)
  • 2344235 - Consulting: Handling Data Archiving Processes during systemcopies

For a BW system with ODP source systems it is strongly recommended to use the Post Copy Automation Tool.

entry.title: Re: any alternative FM to show removed device
entry.content:
hey ricky_shaw 

almost 10 years but anyway, I am hitting the same problem as you did:  so how am I able to create a link between removed devices and installations?!?

Did you get this ever sorted out?

entry.title: Re: Inventory should be blocked at the delivery and not a...
entry.content:
Hi,

or course it depends on configuration like ATP scope of check and other settings in your system how exactly you experience the process, especially in OnPremise/Private Cloud where you have a lot of options when it comes to customizing and enhancements.

When batches are determined a simulative ATP check is executed. Once the batch is chosen, the batch information is persisted on delivery item level and hence, respected in concurrent ATP checks. However, there is a timeframe between triggering the batch determination and really choosing it, where concurrent check could influence the available quantity as it is not yet in the delivery item.

Another aspect is that in WM the batches of delivery might not be considered, depending on lived processes, as the warehouse efficiency is treated higher. This is, however, not a field for ATP then.

Regards,
Andreas

entry.title: Re: can u share your resolution on the error
entry.content: For what exactly? The body of the question is empty.

entry.title: Virtual: Annual NZ Payroll User Group Meeting
entry.content:
Come together with us to connect with other payroll experts and keep up-to-date with the newest trends in the payroll industry. This year's NZ Payroll User Group Virtual Meeting is set to be both informative and interactive, offering essential updates and networking possibilities that are too good to pass up.

Agenda at a glance:
SAP Update – SAP New Zealand
Payroll Continuous Improvements – EPI-USE
Navigating Payroll Compliance – EPI-USE Labs

Speaker:
Katherine Truman, Localization Product Manager, SAP Globalization

For more information and details about the agenda, please visit here.

REGISTRATION LINK

Attend our onsite events:
- Annual NZ Payroll User Group Meeting in Wellington, 20 February, 2:00 - 5:00 pm. Te Wharewaka, 2 Taranaki Street, Wellington) Link
- Annual NZ Payroll User Group Meeting in Auckland: 21 February, 9:00 - 12:00 pm at SAP (Level 15/151 Queen Street, Auckland Central) Link

If you want to get more information about the New Zealand Payroll topics provided by SAP experts, join the New Zealand Payroll - Human Capital Management Localization Work Zone group.

Hi @Timothy_Dev 
The package  /LRN/S4D400_EXERCISE is only available in our official practice systems: Hands-on Practice for Basic ABAP Programming
Are you using this system?
Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: SAP Test Automation – What tools are available?
entry.content:
Some of you are probably familiar with SAP Enterprise Continuous Testing (ECT) or also known using the name Tricentis Tosca. This tool is already on the market for several years and it is well known with the test automation community. It can be fully integrated with Solution Manger and there is also lite version available named “Tricentis Test Automation integrated with SAP Solution Manager”. The lite version is still fairly powerful, and it comes at no cost for customers, that do have Enterprise Support agreements with SAP.

Some while ago SAP in partnership with Tricentis have issued a new cloud offering called “Tricentis Test Automation integrated with SAP Cloud ALM”, I will refer to “TTA with CALM” in my blog to make it simpler. Some people also refer to this product as Tosca Cloud. But it isn’t the official naming used by neither SAP, neither Tricentis. This comes also free of charge for customers with Enterprise Support agreements. However, based on our experience this also comes without any costs to customers with Preferred Success and possibly to all customers using SAP Cloud ALM. However, I’m unable to verify this statement. And by the way SAP Cloud ALM is a very powerful tool and lot of our customers are unaware that they are eligible to use it free of charge as long as they have any Cloud product from SAP. I really recommend checking the eligibility with your SAP Account Executive.

Very recently SAP (I believe some time in 2024) SAP has also released a paid version called “SAP Test Automation by Tricentis (STA)”.

The below picture nicely depicts different product options and some high-level limitations.

 

 

 

Lot of customers are asking us for an advice on what tool do we recommend. Whether they should opt for the on-premise SAP ECT (Tosca) or they should go for SAP STA (Cloud Tosca) integrated with SAP Cloud ALM. Or their free of charge alternatives TTA

This isn’t really an easy question. However we have recently had a chance to work with the latest Cloud version, so I could provide my angle.

 

Firstly, I have to say that the cloud version is getting constant updates with every single quarter. And really, I believe SAP and Tricentis are investing a lot in order to make both tools comparable. The question we often get is, whether STA is on par with ECT. Well, a huge progress was recently made when STA & TTA with CALM also natively support automation of SAP GUI. Unfortunately, previously only automation of SAP Fiori was possible, which obviously had a bit of limited use for SAP customers, as many of them are in sort of hybrid phase and rely both on Fiori and GUI still.

 

Below picture illustrates STA roadmap in 2024 and beginning of 2025

 

 

If you need to decide on which tool you as organization want to use. You have to first understand what applications and process you want to test with test automation solution. Potentially there is also fifth SAP option and that is applicable for customers on S/4 HANA Public Cloud. If you run on S/4 HANA Public Cloud, then you can use so call „S4 HANA Cloud Test Automation Tool“.  Maybe at some point this offering will contain rich features, I believe at this stage it provides limited functions. It can be nicely connected with Post Upgrade Tests, which from my point of view is really nice. However the inability to test Side-by-Side extensions makes the usability extremely limited to core proceses.

 

 

So back to the original question on what tool to select. If your landscape is predominantly cloud based with web UIs, and you use or plan to utilize SAP Cloud ALM then simply go for the “STA or TTA integrated with SAP Cloud ALM”. If your goal is to also automate non-SAP applications and non-Web based UIs, then simply go for SAP ECT (Tricentis Tosca).

Probably it is worth to consider following aspects:

 

There are however some limitations for TTA/STA developers, but I will summarize them in a different blog post.

SAP Cloud ALM test automation tool for SAP S/4HANA Cloud SOLMAN Test Suite SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery

entry.title: Re: From User Exits to RAP: Adopting a Clean Core Approach in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Thanks for this great post.

RAP Local events is a very powerful tool that can help us leveraging situations where there's not a suitable Badi or when the client doesn't want to pay for Even Mesh.

Though, as per my experience, there's a big indirect flaw in RAP Local events related to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The problem is that we can save logs using CL_BALI_LOG_DB but there's no App to display those logs, which doesn't make any sense at all. We can see SAP's reply on the following Question I created on the community: https://community.sap.com/t5/general-and-cross-topics-q-a/is-it-possible-to-display-custom-application-logs-in-s-4hana-cloud-public/qaq-p/14008266/comment-id/17362#M17362 

We have used tcode SLG1 for decades to display our program logs, but now, in Cloud Public, because of some manager's decision, suddenly we can't. Man, that's so frustrating.

(6 attempts) without proper materials?

TIm

entry.title: How to attach FE OP Controller Extension function to OData V4 dataReceived event?
entry.content:
I want to catch the dataReceived event of OData V4 model which is used by a Fiori Elements Standard Object Page in a Controller Extension. So that after every time the data is loaded or refreshed on the screen, I get into my custom Controller Extension function.

At onAfterRendering the data is not yet available.
So I have to attach an event to the model.

When I tried this

 

onAfterRendering: function () {
  var oModel = this.base.getExtensionAPI().getModel();
  oModel.attachEvent('dataReceived', myFunction);
},

 

I saw that only events messageChange and sessionTimeout are available in the model.

So maybe they are available after the ExtensionAPIPromise, but it the function is not called.

 

onInit: function () {
  var oView = this.getView();
  ExtensionAPI.getExtensionAPIPromise(oView).then(
    function (oExtensionAPI) {
      oExtensionAPI.attachPageDataLoaded(function (event) {
        var sPath = event.context.sPath;  // get the path
        var oData = event.context.getModel().getProperty(sPath); 
      }.bind(this));
    }.bind(this)
  )
},

 

I also tried this. but the event is not triggered. Probably because it creates a new binding.

onAfterRendering: function () {
  var oModel = this.base.getExtensionAPI().getModel();
  var oContextBinding = oModel.bindContext("/Run");
  oContextBinding.attachEvent('dataReceived', function (event) { 
    console.log('Testing...')
  })
},

Could you help me?

entry.title: SAC - Advanced Formula Data Action - Calculate Prior Previous Year Ending Balances.
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

 

We are currently facing a challenge in SAP Analytics Cloud concerning data action using advanced formulas, specifically regarding the calculation of previous years' figures within the Account and Date dimensions. We are utilizing an Account-Based Model perform forecasts on our Balance Sheet Model.

 

Requirement:

Our objective is to calculate the Prior Year Average Balance for future periods based on actual data. The formula we would like to implement is as follows:

=(PY-1 End Bal + PY End Bal)/2

 Example:

For example, when we are on **January 2025**, our calculation should reference the following values:

 

If I am in any month in any month of current year, it has to pick last year Dec Ending values of Accounts Payable data from Account Dimension – Prior Previous Year Dec Data for Account Payable data from Account Dimension.

 

Chart:

 

2023.Dec

2024.Jan

2024.Feb

2024.Mar

2024.Apr

2024.May

2024.Jun

2024.Jul

2024.Aug

2024.Sep

2024.Oct

2024.Nov

2024.Dec

2025.Jan

10

12

13

15

15

15

20

20

25

25

30

35

40

50 (Expected)

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

- **2024 Dec** (Current Year End Balance)

- **2023 Dec** (Prior Year End Balance)

 

Thus, the expected result for **2025 Jan** would be:

On Jan 2025 When I run data action it should calculate : =(PY-1 End Bal + PY End Bal)

2024.Dec + 2023

40               +    10  Expected Results = 50 on 2025.Jan

 

 

Current Approach:

At present, I am utilizing the `PREYEARLAST` function to retrieve last year's ending data. However, I am encountering difficulties in formulating a straightforward syntax to fetch the data from the Prior Previous Year.

 

To address this, I have implemented the following workaround, although I have concerns regarding its efficiency:

 

IF [d/Date].[p/MONTHDESC] = "Jan" THEN

  DATA([d/Account]="Stat_PYAvgBal_AP") =   (RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account]="Stat_AP", [d/Date]=PREYEARLAST([d/Date])) +

    RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account]="Stat_AP", [d/Date]=PREVIOUS(13, "MONTH", [d/Date]))) / 2

```

 

This approach retrieves data for all 12 months of the current year while going back 24 months, which may not be optimal.

 

Request for Assistance:

I would greatly appreciate any insights or recommendations on achieving this calculation in a more efficient manner. Your expertise in this area would be invaluable.

 

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

 

Regards,

Ramesh

entry.title: Value Accelerator Event - Structure question
entry.content:
Dear Community, I am reaching out to you because I am trying to understand how one of the events that I have in a Value Accelerator.

This event is suppose to materialize the Supplier invoice due date. 

Here is the code associated : 

 

SELECT
c_caseid,
c_time,
c_eventname,
"Number of Invoice Line Items",
"Accounting Document Primary Key"
FROM (

SELECT
CONCAT(bseg_bkpf.mandt, bseg_bkpf.bukrs, bseg_bkpf.gjahr, bseg_bkpf.belnr) AS c_caseid,
CASE -- always adding 23:59:59 as time
WHEN COALESCE(zbd3t, 0) > 0 THEN DATE_ADD('day', CAST(zbd3t AS INT) ,from_unixtime(zfbdt/1000 + 86399))
WHEN COALESCE(zbd3t, 0) = 0 AND COALESCE(zbd2t, 0) > 0 THEN DATE_ADD('day', CAST(zbd2t AS INT) ,from_unixtime(zfbdt/1000 + 86399))
WHEN COALESCE(zbd3t, 0) = 0 AND COALESCE(zbd2t, 0) = 0 AND COALESCE(zbd1t, 0) > 0 THEN DATE_ADD('day', CAST(zbd1t AS INT) ,from_unixtime(zfbdt/1000 + 86399))
ELSE from_unixtime(zfbdt / 1000 + 86399)
END AS c_time,
'Due Date passed' AS c_eventname,
COUNT(bseg_bkpf.buzei) OVER (PARTITION BY bseg_bkpf.belnr) AS "Number of Invoice Line Items",
bseg_bkpf.bkpf_c_key AS "Accounting Document Primary Key"
FROM bseg_bkpf
WHERE zfbdt IS NOT NULL
AND (bseg_bkpf.tcode != 'MR8M' OR bseg_bkpf.tcode IS NULL)
AND bschl IN (
-- Invoices
'31', -- Vendor Invoices
'22', -- Reverse Invoice
'21', -- Credit Memos
'32' -- Reverse Credit Memo
)
)

WHERE c_eventname IS NOT NULL
AND c_caseid IS NOT NULL
AND c_time IS NOT NULL

 

What I am not sure to understand is that it seems we are adding a timestamp to the due date but I don't get why as if the invoice is due today : then it is easy to determine just using the date if the invoice is overdue or not. 

Any idea ???

Thank you

Sanddie

entry.title: SFX Masterclass Pilot in LATAM-Brasil–Platform

The SAP SuccessFactors SFX Global Masterclass Program is pleased to announce pilot sessions for customers in Latin America (LATAM/LAC).

  • Title: SFX Masterclass – Platform
  • Date: 3/10/2025 to 3/14/2025
  • Region - MKU: LATAM - Brasil

Please find more information and links to register here (*SAP SuccessFactors customer authentication required*): https://community.sap.com/t5/product-and-customer-updates/sap-successfactors-sfx-global-masterclass-program-pilot-for-latin-america/ba-p/14016362

entry.title: SAP just launched the Business Suite - what does this mean for Finance?
entry.content:
Feb 13, 2025 is “Business Unleashed Day” for the SAP Community, with announcements on SAP Business Suite and SAP Business Data Cloud - and how AI, data, and applications are combined like never before to help you unleash your full potential and make you unstoppable.

Whether your company has been running an SAP ERP solution for many years, or whether you’re just at the beginning of the evaluation of a Cloud ERP solution, you might be wondering what impact these announcements – especially the announcements of the SAP Business Suite - will have on your landscape planning.

Evolution from monolithic to composable

Just as technology has completely changed since ERP was first defined for 1970s mainframes, SAP’s approach to ERP has evolved to keep up you keep us with the needs of your business:

  • Modular capabilities that can be adopted quickly as new needs arise
  • Always up-to-date software-as-a-service solutions that give you non-disruptive access to innovations
  • Clear interoperability and data semantics so that you will be in the best position to realize the benefits of AI

The 2025 Gartner® ERP Primer for 2025 states that “in Gartner’s 2023 ERP, Procurement, HCM and Finance Apps Survey of those manager level and above who are responsible for ERP decision making, 71% reported their organizations were either in process or have plans to adopt a flexible and agile ERP architecture within two years.”

So, chances are, you’re already thinking about the importance of having the flexibility to choose business technology more flexibly to support your growth initiatives.

 

 

Gartner: ERP Primer for 2025, by Denis Torii, Dixie John. GARTNER is a registered trademark and service mark of Gartner, Inc. and/or its affiliates in the U.S. and internationally and is used herein with permission. All rights reserved.

 

The SAP Business Suite is SAP’s answer to Composable ERP

You know SAP as a leader in Cloud ERP for years, but the “ERP and Finance” focus of finance teams may overlook the additional, incremental value SAP can provide with our broader family of solutions – solutions that address business needs and can be adopted incrementally, like Fieldglass, Ariba, SuccessFactors, Concur, and CX.

Since the interoperability and data semantics among the applications is curated by SAP, the insights that can be achieved with SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP Business AI are limitless.

 

RISE with SAP is a transformation journey aimed at helping SAP ERP customers transition to the SAP Business Suite - so they can stay ahead of the competition by continuously innovating – across their whole enterprise landscape – not just in “ERP”.

What this means is that Finance teams should be thinking of the value that SAP solutions can create in your IT landscape. For example, adopting updated, world-class procurement processes have benefits specifically for the CFO in terms of cash visibility, risk mitigation and more. You can read a great blog on the topic here.

The SAP Business Suite approach encourages companies to think beyond their administrative ERP footprint: how can we adopt the best solutions to solve all types of business problems – for the Chief Revenue Officer, or for the Chief Human Resources Officer?

Cloud software and software-as-a-service promised that business teams would be able to pick-and-choose the services that meet their business requirements. However, often IT does have to be involved to manage integrations and ensure data interoperability. The SAP Business Suite aims to make that modularity - and business user choice - promise a reality.  

And modularity is not just part of the North Star architecture at the line of business level. SAP Business Suite is a reminder that within the line of business solutions, there are software-as-a-service solutions that can be adopted to solve specific business problems – even if your current core architecture might be more monolithic than you’d like.

Composability within Financial Management

Even within the areas that are traditionally considered to be “ERP”, SAP is offering more flexibility and modularity than ever before. Based on your business needs, these SaaS solutions – with their out-of-the-box best practices and constant access to innovation - are available as extensions to SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud:

  • SAP Green Ledger - to align your carbon and financial accounting by integrating carbon emissions with your business processes, complementing SAP’s portfolio of sustainability solutions, including SAP Sustainability Control Tower.
  • SAP Advanced Financial Closing – for orchestrating financial closing activities across ERP systems
  • SAP Account Substantiation and Automation by BlackLine – to automate manual closing tasks and reconciliations
  • SAP Digital Banking – to automate connections to any financial service provider (banks, SWIFT, payment service providers) in a quick and easy way for more control
  • SAP Cloud for Credit Integration – to integrate and manage external credit rating information for completely touchless, responsive credit decisionmaking
  • SAP Trading Platform Integration – to connect to leading trading platform providers for inbound and outbound straight-through-processing of trades 
  • SAP Risk and Assurance Management - to automate compliance checks and manage remediations efficiently

These solutions – that solve specific business problems- are integrated with our standard financial management processes in Cloud ERP.

So, if you’re part of the 70% of ERP leaders thinking about flexibility in ERP, you should consider what the SAP Business Suite can do for your organization. Making the most of the SAP Business Suite will help you

  • Manage uncertainty through effective steering and agile planning
  • Navigate transformation supported by efficient processes to balance growth and profitability
  • Enable compliance and sustainability in an ever-evolving regulatory and risk environment

Learn more about Finance solutions here.

entry.title: Integration Advisor 1:1 mapping and
entry.content:
Hi experts,

is it possible to create a 1:1 mapping in Integration Advisor and change only one Field on the Target Side.
In SAP PO graphical mapping it was possible to click on a button an the mapping was created.

Or is it possible to use a custom XSLT.

 

the xsl code works fine, but i want to add all other fields an sections 1:1 as easy as possible.

kind regards.

You can leverage context based adaptation to hide certain UIBB's based on user roles.

Regards

Anirban

entry.content:

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Mass Upload HSN/SAC Code in SAP S/4HANA Public cloud
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community, 

 

You can refer to the SAP Help Portal Documentation below in order to learn more about the process of Mass Uploading HSN/SAC code

Mass Upload HSN/ SAC

 

I hope this helps!

 

Best Regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: International Editable SALV Day 2025...
entry.content:
I'm in love with CL_SALV and I make all efforts available to prevent users to ask for editable fields. And when they do, I usually add a hotspot in the cell that leads to a popup. CL_GUI did never deserved all the effort you must put in their events to prevent errors and control changes.

One of my biggest fears is to see "editability" implemented in CL_SALV_TABLE. Because they will implement it the same way they "improved" this site.

Yes, now you are able to edit those cells, but to grant you that, we had to prevent hotspots, colors and even custom status bars and functions. But don't worry, we are working hard to fix that, and we plan to release it just the day after the "follow" feature in our wonderful site.

Because they are SAP, and they care about things we cannot even fathom.

entry.title: Re: Burst a story using a condition in SAC
entry.content:
I was able to achieve this by creating a story with Book marks. The story has an input control that allows you to select a value for filtering the measure, say Sales in the story so that when you create a scheduled publication, I can select the book mark that contains the selection (in the input control for ex, sales >=10000). For this I did not have to use the 'Burst' option but the usual 'Broadcast' option worked!.

However, now I want the email to be triggered to the intended recipients only if the condition is true for the particular interval (day, or week or month) in the story. If the condition is not satisfied, then no email should be triggered. Could anyone please provide an answer to this?

 

Regards,

entry.title: Create an error dtp on a non-cumulative cube
entry.content:
Hi guys

It is possilbe to create an error DTP for a non a non-cumulative cube?.

I'm extracting data from a non cumulative ADSO to another non cumulative ADSO, however I'm getting the following error:  

Time inconsistency detected for record 999999

Message number: RSDSO_UPDATE006

So my idea was create an error DTP to catch the incorrect record.  Any advise?

Regards Oscar

entry.title: Re: How to redeem a campaign code to get a free SAP Certification exam attempt and schedule your exa
entry.content:
Hello, @David_Chaviano 

Thank you for all your help towards us. 

I just have one question, I have already purchased (with the campaign code) the certification attempt, but I am having trouble accessing the Learning Hub material to study and get all the concepts before scheduling my Exam. How can I access SAP Learning Hub? Is it possible with the One Attempt Certification Code I got? Or do I need to pay for that access? I kindly request your help to understand where I can study for the exam.

Kind regards,

Isabel

entry.title: Can we assign multiple freight orders to a single delivery ?
entry.content:
Hi all 

 

Can we assign multiple freight orders to a single delivery ? As we have a requirement to settle down the fright cost by leg , means by stage.

entry.title: HANA Cloud - cannot change Update Mode
entry.content: I am currently running two versions of HANA Cloud QRC3/2024 and QRC4/2024.  I would like to change the default Update Mode of the instance to "Apply changes without a restart".  I can view the current default mode in SAP HANA Cloud Central's Overview -> Configuration page "Apply changes with a restart".  However when I click on Manage Configuration (upper right section of page), the "General" or any other page that appears does not have the Update Mode section to change.  From a security perspective, I have SAP HANA Cloud Administrator, SAP HAAN Cloud Security Adminstrator and Subaccount Administrator role collections.   I created the HANA Cloud instance so I would think I would be able to change the Update Mode.   I would appreciate any assistance that can be provided.

entry.title: Re: Community Member Spotlight: Roberta Fernandes - February 2025 Edition
entry.content: Excellent.  She is a nice friend and a very active person in the Brazil SAP community

entry.title: Don't Miss These SAP Commerce Cloud Live Sessions! Key Dates & Insights (February 18-28, 2025)
entry.content:
Enhance Your Technical Skills with Our Scripting Support Deep Dive in SAP Commerce Cloud! | February 18, 2025

  • Join us for an expert-led session covering ImpEx, Cronjobs, event handling, task engines, and dynamic business process management. Gain real-time insights from experts during an interactive Q&A session designed to enhance your SAP Commerce Cloud capabilities. Don't miss out—secure your spot today: Register Now
  • If you want to first taste of using Scripts, check the step-by-step Guide to Implementing CronJobs in SAP Commerce Cloud Using Scripting
  • 🌟 This session is part of our series of 9 Expert Deep Dive Live Sessions, unlocking the technical essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud. Check out the other exciting sessions available: Explore More Live Sessions

Enhance Your SAP Commerce Cloud Administration Skills: Logging & Monitoring | February 18, 2025

  • Join our expert live session in the SAP Commerce Cloud System Administrator series, where we dive deep into essential tools for effective system management.
    • Access log traces for Commerce Solutions through the Cloud Logging System (CLS)
    • Utilize logs to diagnose issues and establish alerts for proactive management
    • Leverage Application Performance Monitoring (APM) with Dynatrace to oversee various components
    • Register now to secure your spot
  • Note: This session is part of our broader series tailored for SAP Commerce Cloud system administrators. Explore other valuable sessions to complete your learning journey here.

Join Us Live: SAP Customer Data Cloud & Commerce Cloud Integration Essentials | February 18, 2025

  • In this live session, you will explore the integration between SAP Customer Data Cloud and SAP Commerce Cloud.
  • By the end of this live session, you will be able to:
    • Configure the integration between SAP Customer Data Cloud, SAP Commerce Cloud, and Composable Storefront
    • Navigate through the integration codes and setup configurations
    • Validate the integration flows and features within SAP Customer Data Cloud, SAP Commerce Cloud, and Composable Storefront
  • Register now!

Unlock the Power of Backoffice Access Management in SAP Commerce Cloud! | February 19, 2025

  • Join our Expert Deep Dive session to delve into the capabilities of Backoffice user roles and authority groups. Learn how to customize application mash-ups tailored for various Backoffice users. Don’t miss out—secure your spot today: Register Now
  • For a sneak peek, check out a simple application explanation here: Quick Preview
  • ✨ This session is part of our 9-session series crafted to help you fully leverage the capabilities of SAP Commerce Cloud. Discover more by diving into our other insightful sessions: Explore More Technical Sessions

Exclusive live session to explore Server Side Rendering (SSR) in SAP Commerce Cloud composable storefront | February 20, 2025

  • Join our exclusive live session to explore the power of Server Side Rendering (SSR) in SAP Commerce Cloud composable storefront.
  • What you'll learn:
    • The importance of SSR for your business
    • How to configure, build, and run SSR effectively
    • Unleashing the potential of SSR in composable storefronts
    • Coding best practices for perfection
    • Expert tips for troubleshooting and optimization 
  • Elevate your skills and transform your storefront experience! 🌟 Register now: Register Here
  • Note: This session is part of our 9-session Expert Deep Dive series on the technical essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud. Explore more sessions here: Explore Other Sessions

Identify Integrations and Data Management in SAP Commerce Cloud for System Admins | February 20, 2025

  • Join us for the fourth installment in our SAP Commerce Cloud System Administrator series, focusing on Integrations and Data Management.
  • Key topics covered:
    • Discover integration options and tools available for SAP Commerce Cloud
    • Implement effective data management strategies
    • Learn how to create and restore snapshots with ease
  • Secure your spot today
  • Reminder: This session is part of our comprehensive series designed for SAP Commerce Cloud system administrators. Please check the entire series for a complete skill set here.

Boost Your Skills with Our SAP Commerce Cloud Certification Prep Live Session for Business Users! | February 25, 2025

  • 🌟Enhance your certification journey with our 1-hour live session, crafted to prepare you for the SAP Certified Application Associate - SAP Commerce Cloud Business User exam. Delve into essential topics and receive strategic preparation guidance tailored for business and technology consultants, software architects, and more.
  • 👉Register now: Business User Certification Preparation Session
  • 📌If you're seeking support for the Commerce Cloud Developer certification, explore our another dedicated session here: Developer Certification Preparation Session

Unlock the Power of SAP Commerce Cloud with Our Introductory Live Session  | February 26, 2025

  • Join us for a dynamic, 1-hour session designed to introduce you to the essential features of SAP Commerce Cloud. Perfect for newcomers or those needing a refresher, this session focuses on foundational capabilities and their application in supporting e-commerce solutions. Gain a confident understanding of the core functionalities and learn to navigate the interface with ease.
  • 🌐Learning Objectives:
    • Describe the core features and functionalities of SAP Commerce Cloud
    • Navigate the SAP Commerce Cloud interface with confidence
    • Identify key components that support e-commerce solutions within the system
  • Don't miss this opportunity to build your SAP Commerce Cloud knowledge from the ground up and maximize its potential for your e-commerce projects.
  • 👉Register here to secure your spot!

Advance Your Career with SAP Commerce Cloud Developer Certification Preparation Live Session | February 28, 2025

  • 🌟Elevate your SAP skills with our dedicated 1-hour live session, crafted to prepare you for the SAP Certified Development Professional - SAP Commerce Cloud Developer exam. Specifically designed for Software Developers, Architects, and Technology Consultants, this session focuses on critical exam topics and offers expert strategies for building a strong study plan. Enhance your professional-level certification journey and secure your success!
  • 👉Register now: Live Session for Developer Certification Preparation
  • 📌If you're seeking support for the Business User Certification exam, explore our other live session here: Live Session for Business User Certification Preparation

entry.title: Re: Understanding DIV and MOD in ABAP and beyond
entry.content:
Thanks for sharing! Wow, that was quite an excursion into what I thought was a simple subject covered in regular school Math. 🙂

If anyone is still confused, very simple way to remember this is using an example I learned in the very first SAP project. You're selling bottled drinks as single bottles and also cases of 12 bottles (typical case of 750 ml wine bottles). If a customer orders 15 bottles, you'll send them 1 full case (12 bottles) and 3 single bottles.

So, DIV (15,12) will give you number of full cases and MOD will give you number of additional single bottles. Cheers!

entry.title: Product Compliance (new): automatic substance checks not starting with uploaded materials
entry.content:
Dear Community

Issue with SAP S/4HANA for Product Compliance (on-prem, release 2023), Product Marketability and Chemical Compliance solution capability.

Solution went live a month ago, with the first bundle of products/materials views (Raw and Unpacked) being created "by hand". 

As this process is very time consuming we are mass uploading materials data via Migration Cockpit. All is well with a major exception: the automatic substance checks, from the compliance requirements such as EU REACH SVHC Status, are not starting and thus, the compliance assessments are not performed.

I'm uploading the data with this sequence:

1. Bulk Raw Material Compliance Info -> 2. (related) Packaged Raw Material Compliance Info -> 3. (Bulk) Product Compliance Info -> 4. (related) Packaged Product Compliance Info (if necessary*) - 5. Analytical Composition 

* When uploading the Raw Material Compliance Info, if the logistic role of "sold" is marked, the Unpacked Compliance info is created.

For the time being we are letting out the materials that (will) have a material based composition (as these are few with my client).

Once again, I kindly ask for your advice and help

 

Paulo Pinto

entry.title: Re: Understanding DIV and MOD in ABAP and beyond
entry.content: Yes, but the real fun begins, when the numbers are negative ;-). Can you extend your bottles example to negative bottle debts and get the ABAP results?
